slice.name: first-team-admin-passwordless-sign-in
slice.scope: ResearchOps-owned passwordless sign-in, account dashboard and Team Admin role-assignment access

user_goal: Allow the first Team Admin to sign in with a one-time code, reach a signed-in account dashboard, sign out and access Team Admin role assignment controls.
key_decisions: Keep the ResearchOps user in ResearchOps UI rather than exposing Cloudflare Access UI.
Use the preview Worker API origin for branch-preview passwordless endpoints after the Pages same-origin proxy proved unreliable for this branch.
Redirect successful sign-in and already-authenticated visits from /pages/account/sign-in/ to /pages/account/.
Render account actions from capability codes such as role.assign, governed.approve, safeguarding.view and audit.view rather than role labels.
Address automated security review by enforcing one-time-code attempt limits and filtering expired role assignments from passwordless session roles and permissions.
manual_verification: /pages/account/sign-in/ sends a one-time code by email.
The dashboard shows active team and current roles.
Permission-based actions appear from capability codes, not role labels.
Sign out returns the user to /pages/account/sign-in/.
Visiting /pages/account/sign-in/ while already signed in redirects back to /pages/account/.
The Team Admin can open /pages/team/role-assignments/ and see the assign-a-role form.
boundaries: No user-facing account request route exists yet.
Role assignment final submit has not been manually tested because the preview environment only contains the first Team Admin account.
Production passwordless deployment remains separate from this preview validation path.

codex_review_fixes.title: Enforce attempt limits before verifying codes
codex_review_fixes.fix: The passwordless verifier now checks attempts_remaining before hash comparison and locks challenges when the final permitted attempt is consumed.

codex_review_fixes.title: Exclude expired role assignments from permissions
codex_review_fixes.fix: Passwordless roles and permissions now filter active assignments by expires_at so expired sensitive access no longer authorises /api/me contexts.
